Front-End Developer &
Shopify Solutions Engineer

I build clean, responsive websites and help eCommerce brands solve tricky front-end, cart, and app integration issues.

View Work Read Blog Work With Me
ShopifyJavaScriptReactWordPressTechnical Support
About

A bit about me

I got into web development around 2014, mostly by pulling apart WordPress themes to see what broke. That trial-and-error time with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript stuck with me longer than any tutorial did.

Since then I've worked with clients, startups, and a Shopify apps company, spending a lot of time at the intersection of front-end code and merchant support. A good chunk of that was diagnosing Shopify storefront issues: cart drawer conflicts, broken app embeds, checkout quirks, and the kind of bugs that only appear on one browser at one screen size.

What I find genuinely interesting is the gap between "the error exists" and "the merchant understands what happened and can move forward." That translation layer from stack trace to clear next step is something I've gotten a lot of practice at.

Outside of client work, I'm building out a set of resources: debugging checklists, reusable snippets, and practical guides aimed at the exact problems I've seen come up repeatedly in Shopify and front-end work.

Services

What I Can Help With

From Shopify troubleshooting to full front-end builds — here is where I can add value.

🛒

Shopify Troubleshooting

Diagnosing and fixing cart drawer conflicts, app integration issues, theme bugs, and storefront JavaScript errors.

💻

Front-End Development

Building responsive, accessible, and performant websites using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React.

🔧

Cart Drawer & App Integration Fixes

Resolving conflicts between Shopify apps (Candy Rack, Gift Box, etc.) and cart drawer or checkout flows.

🎨

Landing Pages & Portfolio Websites

Designing and developing clean, conversion-focused landing pages and personal portfolio sites.

📝

Technical Documentation & Support Content

Writing clear technical guides, troubleshooting docs, and support articles for developers and merchants.

✨

UX Improvements

Identifying and fixing usability issues on eCommerce storefronts and web applications.

Work

Featured Projects

Selected GitHub projects with live demos and source code.

Candy Duds

A web-based replication of the Candy Rack app, built to showcase pop-ups and special offers. It demonstrates product upsell popups built with React, Tailwind CSS, and styled-components.

ReactTailwind CSSStyled ComponentsVercel
Live Demo Source Code

Blogsy

A Blog RSS engine built with Next.js that fetches and displays RSS feeds in a clean, readable interface.

Next.jsRSSAPIVercel
Live Demo Source Code

Wizionary

An interactive audio player built with React and powered by Vite, featuring playback controls and a responsive layout.

ReactViteWeb AudioVercel
Live Demo
Blog

From the Blog

Practical articles on Shopify, JavaScript, and front-end development.

Shopify New guide

How to Debug Shopify Cart Drawer Issues

A practical walkthrough of the most common cart drawer problems in Shopify themes — from JavaScript conflicts to app embed timing issues — and how to fix them.

Read more →
JavaScript New guide

Useful JavaScript Snippets for Shopify Stores

A collection of reusable JavaScript snippets for common Shopify customizations: cart attribute updates, variant switching, sticky headers, and more.

Read more →
Shopify Field note

Lessons From Supporting Shopify Merchants

What I have learned from troubleshooting hundreds of Shopify support tickets — patterns, common mistakes, and how to communicate technical fixes clearly.

Read more →
Front-End Field note

Building a Fast Portfolio With Modern Front-End Tools

How I rebuilt my portfolio using Astro and Tailwind CSS — the decisions I made, the tradeoffs, and what makes this stack great for developer portfolios.

Read more →
